passages.passage_text: Directions (Q. 1-9): Study the following information and answer the questions given below: In a certain code, the symbol for 0 (zero) is * and that for 1 is ∆. There are no other symbols for any other numbers greater than one. The numbers greater than 1 are to be written only by using the two symbols given above. The value of the symbol for 1 doubles itself every time it shifts one place to the left. Study the following examples: '0' is written as * '1' is written as ∆ '2' is written as ∆* '3' is written as ∆∆ '4' is written as ∆** and so on.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Which of the following numbers will be represented by ∆∆*∆?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Which of the following will represent the multiplication of *∆ and ∆∆?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Which of the following will represent 8 + 5 + 2 + 3?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Which of the following will represent the HCF of 10, 20 and 15?
